The final principle for us to fully understand is perhaps the most primitive because even single-celled organisms are affected by it. It's the principle of scarcity, which states that opportunities appear more valuable when they become less available. In Dr. Nidert's core motives model, scarcity fits in, along with consistency, as a principle that increases your persuasive success by spurring people to respond in your direction, not just emotionally or cognitively, but behaviorally.
